groups.text: It is currently 22 degrees and cloudy in Berlin-Tempelhof.
With the morning weather set, here is the news.

Spain is reeling from a historic heatwave that claimed more than one thousand lives in June. This toll is more than double the four hundred seven deaths recorded during the previous record-breaking June of 2025, contributing to the hottest first six months ever documented for the country. In other news, Williams Racing plans to debut an almost entirely new car by the Azerbaijan Grand Prix in late September. Team principal James Vowles confirmed the upgrade to the FW48 aims to reduce weight and improve mechanical performance to lift the team from eighth place in the Constructors’ Championship. Meanwhile, in open source software, a new pull request for PyTorch introduces the first part of the switch HOP, known as gen_schema. This change allows all operands to be in-place mutated and was authored with assistance from the AI coding assistant Claude.

Ukrainian forces are conducting daily strikes on military and logistical targets in Crimea to further isolate the occupied peninsula. For pro-Ukrainian residents living under Russian occupation, these attacks have sparked hope of a possible deoccupation for the first time since the 2023 counteroffensive. While some acknowledge they remain far from that goal, others are waiting for a total collapse of the occupation regime and the arrival of Ukrainian troops. This tension exists alongside unrelated failures in German sports. Following the national team's exit from the World Cup, reports have emerged regarding Julian Nagelsmann’s leadership. Details suggest a disconnect between his public eloquence and his private conduct, with players claiming he rarely spoke to them individually during the tournament. Some athletes were particularly frustrated that Nagelsmann cycled to training with his wife during the heat of the competition rather than focusing on the squad.

In West Africa, cocoa prices have collapsed from a 2024 peak of nearly 13,000 dollars per ton to around 3,000 dollars as of April 2026. This volatility leaves millions of small farmers in Côte d'Ivoire and Ghana facing poverty; some are forced to pull children from school to harvest more beans just to afford three meals a day, while others see crops rot because they cannot pay workers. In the UK, two police officers face gross misconduct investigations after handcuffing 18-year-old Henry Nowak as he lay dying following a fatal stabbing in December 2025. The watchdog is examining why first aid was bypassed and if an officer dismissed Nowak's injuries. Meanwhile, Ukrainian diplomats are being denied hospital access to victims of a residential explosion in Monaco. Authorities there have not officially confirmed the nationality of those injured, including a woman in critical condition and a boy awaiting police interviews.

Russia has closed seven key railway border crossings with Finland, Estonia, and Latvia as of July 1. This move follows a recent increase in freight tariffs for cargo bound for those countries, though Russia has not provided a formal justification for the closures. While this disrupts logistics along NATO's eastern flank, some market pricing suggests these logistical challenges may actually reduce the probability of Russia capturing Sloviansk or Donetsk Oblast. In a different sphere of international tension, Qatari Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim Al Thani hosted US envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner in Doha. They met to attempt to restart direct negotiations between Washington and Tehran over Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, in South Korea, Lee “Faker” Sang-hyeok recorded a solo kill on Team Liquid’s Quid during the Mid-Season Invitational, fueling betting activity on the decentralized prediction platform Polymarket.

Donald Trump continues to push for restrictions on birthright citizenship for children of illegal migrants, despite a court ruling declaring such measures unconstitutional. This legal clash highlights a broader tension over the malleability of laws established in 1868. Meanwhile, alternative media outlets are amplifying theories that the Covid-19 virus was created via gain-of-function research in an American biolaboratory in Wuhan and subsequently released. These reports suggest such claims were previously suppressed as conspiracy theories, gaining new momentum following disclosures by former intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard. In Germany, a different kind of identity crisis is surfacing. Some observers argue the country has shifted from a globally respected power to a mediocre state. They suggest that even banal events, like the outcome of a football match, now serve as metaphors for a nation struggling to accept its diminished role in the modern world.

Overnight Russian attacks have killed at least three people and injured more than a dozen in southeastern Ukraine. Governor Oleksandr Hanzha reports that drones struck the Dnipropetrovsk region early Wednesday, killing one woman and damaging five petrol stations, while two others died in a separate attack on Zaporizhzhia. In response, President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Ukraine has expanded its long-range drone campaign, targeting an oil refinery in Ufa and a military-industrial facility in the Penza region. Shifting to space, NASA is accelerating plans for a permanent lunar settlement by awarding 590 million dollars to three private companies for cargo and scientific equipment transport. Meanwhile, in the field of artificial intelligence, researchers are addressing prompt injection as a persistent failure mode in tool-using systems. One proposed mitigation uses a middleware layer called Sentinel Gateway to separate trusted instruction channels from untrusted external data to prevent unauthorized execution.

In Venezuela, rescue workers and residents are still digging through rubble in La Guaira to find survivors and recover bodies after two earthquakes struck on June 24. With magnitudes of 7.2 and 7.5, the disaster killed nearly 2,000 people and left tens of thousands missing, marking the worst seismic event for the country in over a century. Meanwhile, Spain is grappling with its hottest first half of the year on record. The Carlos III Health Institute reports that at least 1,028 people died during last month's heatwave, more than double the deaths recorded in June 2025. In contrast to these tragedies, sports fans are celebrating as France cruised into the World Cup round of 16 with a 3-0 win over Sweden, fueled by a brace from Kylian Mbappé. Mexico also continues its perfect run, securing a 2-0 victory against Ecuador for their fourth clean sheet of the tournament.
After the headlines, back to the weather.
This afternoon will be cloudy with rain chances up to 38 percent and temperatures between 21 and 26 degrees.
And from the weather, a quick look at the calendar.

On your calendar today: personal calendar: all day: Haushaltspflege at 🏚️, at 10:00, Optikrunde, at 10:30, Research weekly at Gauss, at 11:00, MxM Spotlight, and at 15:00, Gruppenmeeting Gregor and Research Seminar.
Mail worth checking: Sebastian Sztwiertnia sent you a message in Teams. Also, Gemini sent over the meeting notes from your session with Felix and Patrick.
And that's it for your personal morning broadcast.
[mature adult voice, intensely emotional, tender, radiant; hold a long breath after Rise] Rise... and shine.
[very very long pause before speaking; then mature adult voice, most emotional, strong, warm, radiant, deeply encouraging; hold a long breath after Rise] Rise... and shine.
